help.overview: The Filter menu controls which objects are drawn at all. Toggles are grouped by object type (payloads, debris, rocket bodies), orbital regime (LEO through GEO and beyond), data source, and country, so you can cut the catalog down to exactly the population you are studying.
help.imgAlt: The Filter menu with its grouped visibility toggles
help.imgCaption: Each switch hides or shows a slice of the catalog instantly.
help.howToUse:
1. Open the Filter Menu from the bottom bar or press F.
  2. Toggle the switches; the scene updates immediately as each filter applies.
  3. Click Reset to Defaults to restore the standard view.
help.tip1: Filters combine: an object must pass every active group to stay visible, so an empty looking scene usually means two filters are excluding each other.
help.tip2: The command palette has one click presets such as showing only LEO or hiding debris and rocket bodies.

operationalPayloads.name: Operational Payloads
operationalPayloads.tooltip: Satellites that are currently operational, including those in standby, spare, or extended mission status.

nonOperationalPayloads.name: Non-Operational Payloads
nonOperationalPayloads.tooltip: Satellites that are non-operational or whose status is unknown.

rocketBodies.name: Rocket Bodies
rocketBodies.tooltip: The upper stages of rockets that have delivered payloads to orbit and are now large pieces of space debris.

debris.name: Debris
debris.tooltip: Fragments of spacecraft and rockets, including broken satellites, that are no longer one piece.

unknownType.name: Unknown Type
unknownType.tooltip: Objects in orbit whose purpose or type is not clearly identified yet.

notionalSatellites.name: Notional Satellites
notionalSatellites.tooltip: Satellites that are planned or proposed but not yet launched.

agencies.name: Agencies
agencies.tooltip: Planned feature - This will show agencies on the globe.

groundSensors.name: Ground Sensors
groundSensors.tooltip: Ground-based radar and optical sensors used to track objects in space.

launchFacilities.name: Launch Facilities
launchFacilities.tooltip: Spaceports and launch pads from which rockets are launched.

vleoSatellites.name: vLEO Satellites
vleoSatellites.tooltip: Very Low Earth Orbit satellites with an apogee below 400 km.

leoSatellites.name: LEO Satellites
leoSatellites.tooltip: Low Earth Orbit satellites with an apogee between 400 km and 6000 km.

heoSatellites.name: HEO Satellites
heoSatellites.tooltip: Highly Elliptical Orbit satellites with an eccentricity of 0.1 or greater and an apogee below 39786 km.

meoSatellites.name: MEO Satellites
meoSatellites.tooltip: Medium Earth Orbit satellites with an apogee between 6000 km and 34786 km.

geoSatellites.name: GEO Satellites
geoSatellites.tooltip: Geostationary Orbit satellites with an eccentricity below 0.1 and an apogee between 34786 km and 36786 km.

xgeoSatellites.name: xGEO Satellites
xgeoSatellites.tooltip: Ex-Geosynchronous Orbit satellites with an eccentricity below 0.1 and an apogee above 36786 km, or an eccentricity of 0.1 or greater and an apogee above 39786 km.

vimpelSatellites.name: Vimpel Satellites
vimpelSatellites.tooltip: Vimpel satellite catalog is maintained by the Russian joint-stock company JSC Vimpel, in cooperation with the Keldysh Institute of Applied Mathematics.

celestrakSatellites.name: Celestrak Satellites
celestrakSatellites.tooltip: Celestrak is a popular source for satellite orbital data, providing TLEs for a wide range of satellites and debris.

celestrakSupSatellites.name: Celestrak Supplemental Satellites
celestrakSupSatellites.tooltip: Supplemental satellite data from Celestrak, including additional objects not in the primary catalog.

satnogsSatellites.name: SatNOGS Satellites
satnogsSatellites.tooltip: Satellite data from the SatNOGS network, a community-driven project for satellite observation and tracking.

unitedStates.name: United States
unitedStates.tooltip: Includes satellites from the United States of America.

unitedKingdom.name: United Kingdom
unitedKingdom.tooltip: Includes satellites from the United Kingdom.

ussr.name: USSR
ussr.tooltip: Historical designation for satellites launched by the former Soviet Union.

otherCountries.name: Other Countries
otherCountries.tooltip: Includes satellites from countries not listed above.

starlinkSatellites.name: Starlink Satellites
starlinkSatellites.tooltip: Satellites that are part of SpaceX's Starlink constellation, which aims to provide global broadband internet coverage.
